school: University at Buffalo (SUNY Buffalo) (School of Engineering and Applied Sciences)

majors.name: Aerospace Engineering (BS)
majors.rawMinGpa: 2.5 cumulative GPA (minimum 2.5 in math, science, and major coursework)
majors.rawMinCredits: 12 transferable credits completed after high school
requiredCourses: CHE 107 - General Chemistry for Engineers I (or CHE 101/105)

english.raw: TOEFL: 70 minimum, IELTS: 6.0 minimum, Duolingo: 105 minimum

majors.note: Official requirements from admissions website: https://engineering.buffalo.edu/home/academics/undergrad/admissions/transfer.html. Standard admission requires completion of the four core courses with a combined GPA of at least 2.5, or fast-track with at least two core courses and a combined GPA of at least 3.0.

school: University of Pittsburgh (Swanson School of Engineering)

majors.name: Bioengineering (BS)
majors.rawMinGpa: 3.5 minimum GPA (note: general engineering requires 3.0 minimum GPA, but 3.25 for community colleges)
requiredCourses: CHEM 0110 - General Chemistry I with Lab
CHEM 0120 - General Chemistry II with Lab
PHYS 0174 - Basic Physics for Science and Engineering I (Calculus-based)
PHYS 0175 - Basic Physics for Science and Engineering II (Calculus-based)
recommendedCourses: ENGR 0012 - Introduction to Engineering Computing (C or C++ programming recommended)

english.raw: TOEFL: 80 minimum (95+ competitive), IELTS: 6.5 minimum (7.0+ competitive), Duolingo: 110 minimum (120+ competitive)

majors.note: Official requirements from admissions website: https://www.engineering.pitt.edu/admissions/undergraduate/transfer-students/. Bioengineering has a higher GPA threshold of 3.5. Only courses with a grade of C or better are transferable.
